brief.objective: explain the CARE Coaching Needs Assessment as action-planning support
keyMessages: CCNA identifies strengths, uncovers growth areas, and guides education leaders with action-based data.
CCNA focuses on the work through assessment, data analysis, and action planning.
CCNA gives educators shared, actionable starting points for improving instruction and supporting all learners.
proofPoints: The CCNA service page says CCNA identifies strengths, uncovers growth areas, and guides education leaders with action-based data.
The CCNA service page says the assessment activity focuses on multilingual learner support while also covering broader practices that affect all students.
The CCNA service page contrasts task-oriented data with traditional climate surveys and emphasizes instructional decisions.
brief.cta: Email Yvonne.Williams@wceps.org to discuss your district's CCNA next steps
brief.layoutRecommendation: Use a structured one-pager with a summary column, key message cards, evidence-backed proof points, and a bottom CTA band.

headlineOptions: Action-Based Data for School Improvement Planning
Shared Starting Points for Supporting Multilingual Learners
From Practice Data to Action Planning
subheadOptions: The CARE Coaching Needs Assessment identifies strengths, uncovers growth areas, and guides district EL leaders with action-based data for school improvement and professional development planning.
CCNA helps school teams examine instructional practices, multilingual learner support, and broader routines that affect all students.
Use CCNA to move from assessment and data analysis to action-oriented improvement plans grounded in shared, actionable starting points.
copy.body: CCNA turns school practices into action-based data for improvement planning. The assessment focuses on planning, collaboration, feedback, and leadership routines that directly influence teaching, with focused attention to multilingual learner support and broader practices that affect all students. District EL leaders can use CCNA findings, actionable reporting, prioritized actions, and coaching suggestions to support school improvement and professional development planning.
bullets: Identify strengths and growth areas across instructional practice, collaboration, feedback, and leadership routines.
Collect action-oriented data on practices that affect general education and multilingual learners.
Use interactive reports with prioritized actions and coaching suggestions for PD planning and strategic next steps.
Create shared, actionable starting points for improving instruction and supporting all learners.
Connect assessment, data analysis, and action planning to school improvement priorities.
copy.cta: Email Yvonne.Williams@wceps.org to discuss your district's CCNA next steps

fittedCopy.headline: Action-Based Data for School Improvement Planning
fittedCopy.deck: The CARE Coaching Needs Assessment identifies strengths, uncovers growth areas, and guides district EL leaders.
proofPoints: Use a structured needs assessment to identify strengths and growth.
Use actionable reporting to guide instructional practice.
Connect findings to school improvement and professional development planning.

compositionTemplate.typographyScale: magazine headline, one deck, short proof rail
compositionTemplate.artPlateTreatment: edge-to-edge editorial art column or band
compositionTemplate.copyPlacement: large type block with compact editorial proof band

designRecipe.hierarchy: readable one-pager hierarchy with a headline/deck column, integrated image field, full-width proof rows, and compact CTA
designRecipe.artDirection: premium CCNA education-leadership editorial split for district EL leaders: confident, evidence-based school improvement tone with warm institutional color, inclusive educator/student-adjacent imagery, and subtle data-to-action visual cues
designRecipe.placeholderStrategy: use composition, crop, light, and tonal quiet space to reserve readable app-rendered text areas; do not create fake text, logos, charts, cards, boxes, or UI modules
designRecipe.appComposition: official logo, exact headline, deck, proof rows, and CTA are app-rendered in readable zones; art stays secondary to message clarity
promptDirectives: editorial split one-pager with readable proof-row rhythm
avoid dark poster overlays behind long copy
create visual energy in the image field without fake charts, document modules, or text

compositionScore.question: Would I send this to the WCEPS team without apology?

copyQualityQa.question: Would I send this copy to the WCEPS team without apology?

imageResults.revisedPrompt:
Create a 1024x1536 portrait full-bleed premium education-sector campaign art plate for an app-composed one-pager. Editorial split composition: reserve a calm, readable left-side text zone with tonal quiet space and subtle texture; right side is the main visual field with rich layered depth and visual energy. No typography, no logos, no symbols, no readable marks.

Subject and mood: authentic district school leadership team engaged in a needs-assessment data debrief and action-based school improvement planning conversation for multilingual learner support. The scene should feel professional, practical, evidence-based, and instructional-practice oriented, not generic business analytics or coaching. Show a small diverse education team around a table or collaborative surface reviewing anonymized report pages and assessment reflection materials that contain only blurred/non-readable texture; sticky notes grouped by action priorities with no writing; abstract planning matrix shapes without labels; classroom/school improvement context suggested subtly in the background. Avoid close-up faces and detailed hands; use candid over-shoulder or mid-distance editorial crop.

Brand atmosphere and colors: incorporate CCNA-adjacent palette as environmental accents and lighting only: deep teal #0081A4, aqua #3EB3BD, green #5EA974, warm yellow #F7D470. Warm, confident, clear, school-improvement tone. Premium editorial photography or refined photorealistic/abstract hybrid, polished NY media/ad-agency quality, intentional crop, layered depth, sophisticated lighting, high production value.

Composition constraints: art stays secondary to future app-rendered message. Maintain strong safe margin and quiet contrast zone on left for headline/deck column; lower full-width bands should have subtle visual rhythm suitable for app-rendered proof rows without drawing fake rows or panels. Keep important visual energy away from trim/crop edges. Do not create document layout, cards, panels, blank boxes, UI modules, CTA bars, contact areas, charts, dashboards, icons, labels, seals, badges, wordmarks, chevrons, arrows, mountain-like marks, logo-like marks, or background logo patterns.

Strict text rule: absolutely no readable text, letters, numbers, labels, fake paragraphs, book titles, publisher names, email addresses, URLs, citations, lorem ipsum, or legible chart/dashboard content anywhere. Papers may only have indistinct texture and soft blocks, not readable copy. No fake charts or business graphs. Avoid posed stock workshop scene; avoid generic meeting-table clichés by using intentional editorial crop, atmospheric depth, and authentic school needs-assessment materials.

request.goal: explain the CARE Coaching Needs Assessment as action-planning support
request.topic: action-based data, instructional practice, multilingual learner support, and school improvement planning
request.cta: Email Yvonne.Williams@wceps.org to discuss your district's CCNA next steps
request.notes: Stress needs-assessment support without drifting into general CARE Coaching copy.

sourceEvidence.excerpt: CCNA identifies strengths, uncovers growth areas, and guides education leaders with action-based data to support school improvement and professional development planning.
sourceEvidence.label: CCNA overview

sourceEvidence.label: Shared actionable starting points

sourceEvidence.excerpt: The CCNA assessment activity focuses on multilingual learner support, while also covering broader practices that affect all students.
sourceEvidence.label: Multilingual learner support

sourceEvidence.label: Instructional practice focus
